From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1974 film by Bruno Corbucci

| Il trafficone | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Written by | Bruno Corbucci  [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") |
| Starring | [Carlo Giuffrè](/wiki/Carlo_Giuffr%C3%A8 "Carlo Giuffrè") |
| Cinematography | [Guglielmo Mancori](/wiki/Guglielmo_Mancori "Guglielmo Mancori") |
| Music by | [Ubaldo Continiello](/wiki/Ubaldo_Continiello "Ubaldo Continiello") |
| Release date | * November 28, 1974 (1974-11-28) |
| Running time | 102 minutes |
| Language | Italian |

***Il trafficone*** is a 1974 Italian [commedia sexy all'italiana](/wiki/Commedia_sexy_all%27italiana "Commedia sexy all'italiana") written and directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") and starring [Carlo Giuffrè](/wiki/Carlo_Giuffr%C3%A8 "Carlo Giuffrè").[[1]](#cite_note-70-79_z-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Mereghetti-2)[[3]](#cite_note-stracult-3)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Il_trafficone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

A Neapolitan Vincenzo LoRusso that lives of expedients in Rome, as he tries to sell at an intersection a faux suede jacket, accidentally meets Laura, a beautiful woman who invites him to her house. Between the two it is now a passion, but the unexpected arrival of her husband will be the beginning for Vincenzo a new experience as it will be paid by it to have been caught in bed with his wife, and unlocked it from its psychological impotence. Well briefed by the modern couple of theories of [William Masters and Virginia Johnson](/wiki/William_Masters_and_Virginia_Johnson "William Masters and Virginia Johnson"), though without real expertise will open a sexology center to solve the problems of couples and to earn as much without ever having to make do to support his family.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Il_trafficone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Carlo Giuffrè](/wiki/Carlo_Giuffr%C3%A8 "Carlo Giuffrè"): Vincenzo LoRusso / Dr. Gaetano D'Angelo
* [Marilù Tolo](/wiki/Maril%C3%B9_Tolo "Marilù Tolo"): Rosalia
* [Tina Aumont](/wiki/Tina_Aumont "Tina Aumont"): Laura Vitali
* [Adriana Asti](/wiki/Adriana_Asti "Adriana Asti"): Virginia
* [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale"): Gennaro
* [Rita Calderoni](/wiki/Rita_Calderoni "Rita Calderoni"): Angela, Vincenzo's wife
* [Lino Banfi](/wiki/Lino_Banfi "Lino Banfi"): Luigi Scardocchia
* [Irina Maleeva](/w/index.php?title=Irina_Maleeva&action=edit&redlink=1 "Irina Maleeva (page does not exist)"): Silvana Scardocchia
* [Gianni Agus](/wiki/Gianni_Agus "Gianni Agus"): On. Rivolta
* [Elio Zamuto](/wiki/Elio_Zamuto "Elio Zamuto"): Baron Vito Macaluso
* [Vincenzo Crocitti](/wiki/Vincenzo_Crocitti "Vincenzo Crocitti") : Bastiano
* [Pamela Villoresi](/wiki/Pamela_Villoresi "Pamela Villoresi") : Bastiano's wife
* [Renzo Marignano](/wiki/Renzo_Marignano "Renzo Marignano"): Count Everardo
* [Mino Guerrini](/wiki/Mino_Guerrini "Mino Guerrini"): Judge Filiberto Vettiglia
* [Giancarlo Badessi](/wiki/Giancarlo_Badessi "Giancarlo Badessi") : The Major
* [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") : On. Licanzi
* [Massimo Dapporto](/wiki/Massimo_Dapporto "Massimo Dapporto") : Paziente filmato
